sections.heading: Overview
sections.paragraph: The pharmaceutical landscape is currently defined by a landmark regulatory milestone for proteolysis targeting chimeras, as Arvinas secured FDA approval for VEPPANU (vepdegestrant), the first PROTAC therapy, for ESR1-mutated, ER-positive, HER2-negative advanced breast cancer. This approval, grounded in phase 3 VERITAC-2 trial data demonstrating a median progression-free survival of 5 months versus 2.1 months with fulvestrant, validates the clinical viability of targeted protein degradation. Concurrently, Arvinas presented compelling Phase 1 data for ARV-102, an LRRK2 degrader for Parkinson’s disease, achieving over 50% target reduction in cerebrospinal fluid. Industry analysis further highlights emerging PROTAC candidates targeting BTK, BCL2, and BCL6 in lymphoma, potentially reducing reliance on autologous stem cell transplantation. While specific PROTAC pipeline updates were absent in broader regional reporting, the ecosystem is supported by expanding manufacturing infrastructure and AI-driven discovery tools, such as those highlighted in China’s 2026 budget and Thermo Fisher’s new design centers. These developments collectively position PROTAC technology as a transformative modality across oncology and neurology, despite varying clinical benefit expectations noted in market analysis.

points.text: Arvinas — VEPPANU (vepdegestrant) — ESR1m, ER+/HER2- Advanced Breast Cancer — FDA Approval as first PROTAC therapy
Arvinas — ARV-102 — Parkinson’s Disease — Phase 1 Data showing >50% LRRK2 degradation in CSF
FDA — Guardant360 CDx — Companion diagnostic for ESR1 mutation — Approved alongside vepdegestrant to identify eligible patients
Industry — PROTACs (BTK, BCL2, BCL6) — Lymphoma — Identified as novel degraders reducing ASCT reliance
Arvinas: Clinical-stage biotechnology pioneering PROTAC protein degrader platform for oncology and neurodegenerative diseases.
Vepdegestrant — PROTAC ER degrader — Phase 3 VERITAC-2 — Median PFS 5 months vs 2.1 months (fulvestrant), HR 0.57; ORR 19% vs 4%.
ARV-102 — PROTAC LRRK2 Degrader — Phase 1 — Parkinson’s Disease — 50%+ LRRK2 reduction in CSF, well tolerated, 28-day dosing.
PROTACs (BTK, BCL2, BCL6 degraders) — Degraders — Lymphoma (MCL, DLBCL) — Discussed as emerging classes potentially reducing need for autologous stem cell transplantation.
